hypothesis: Runner-stability hardening becomes safer if any systems-optimizer card that declares `runner_focus`, `failure_mode`, `closed_fail_rule`, or `evidence_commands` is classified as runner-stability even when its id does not use the `sysopt.runner-stability-*` prefix, so naming drift cannot bypass replayable runner-law verification.
measurement: Before the ratchet, `isRunnerStabilityRatchet` checked only `card.id.startsWith('sysopt.runner-stability')`, so kept cards such as `sysopt.runner-stale-cycle-exact-evidence-lock` and `sysopt.runner-observability-tail-lock` still carried `runner_focus`, `failure_mode`, `closed_fail_rule`, and `evidence_commands` yet bypassed `validateRunnerStabilityContract` because their ids drifted to `sysopt.runner-*` without the `-stability-` infix. After the ratchet, `node CLAW/scripts/verify-systems-optimizer.mjs` classifies any card with those runner-stability fields as runner-stability, state policy explicitly requires that field-based classification, and the fixed scope-local promotion bundle stays green.
keep_rule: Keep only if field-bearing runner cards are forced through the runner-stability contract even when their ids drift from `sysopt.runner-stability-*`, and the fixed scope-local evaluation bundle passes without touching route implementation paths.
learning_item: Runner-stability verification must classify by declared runner contract fields, not only by one id prefix; otherwise naming drift can let field-bearing recovery ratchets bypass the very laws they are supposed to prove.
